page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.13xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 52 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8269CY processors. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, each of the 52 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, providing dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.69 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The hardware configuration offers a balanced resource profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution and moderate memory density.

The dedicated core allocation makes this instance type suitable for workloads sensitive to resource contention, such as application servers, containerized microservices, build systems, and medium-scale databases. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration positions it as a compute-and-memory balanced node within a wider distributed architecture.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.13xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 52 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it features a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, ensuring dedicated physical compute resources for each vCPU. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio of 3.69 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This resource profile is well-suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, web serving, container orchestration, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading overhead.
